=== PLAN ===
# sws — Software Wrighter Script Interpreter

Implement a Tcl-like command language interpreter for COR24, written in C
within tc24r's supported subset. The interpreter reads .sws scripts and
provides shell/editor scripting capabilities.

## Language Design
- Command-oriented: everything is a command, words whitespace-separated
- { } blocks for deferred evaluation, " " quoted strings
- $var substitution with $var.field record access
- (cmd args...) parenthesized command substitution
- Comparison via commands (eq, ne, lt, gt, le, ge), not operators
- Structured $rc record for run results (opt-in via pragma run-rc on)
- exists? for safe variable/field presence testing
- Result<Value, ScriptError> error model throughout

## Constraints
- No structs (parallel arrays)
- No malloc/free (static allocation with fixed limits)
- No string library (hand-rolled)
- Single translation unit
- 24-bit int, 8-bit char
- UART I/O only

## Phases
1. Architecture + scaffolding (build/test, main skeleton, string helpers)
2. Tokenizer (whitespace splitting, braces, quotes, comments)
3. Value types (integer, string, record as parallel arrays)
4. Variables (set, $var, $var.field, exists?)
5. Command dispatch (command table, dispatch loop, echo/set/exit)
6. Control flow (if/else, while, comparison/logic commands)
7. Run command (process execution, structured $rc, pragma run-rc on)
8. Filesystem commands (cd, pwd, ls, mkdir, rm, mv, cp, stat)
9. Source command (source file.sws, env get/set/unset)
10. Command substitution ((cmd args...) nested evaluation)
11. Validation (end-to-end testing, error cases, emulator runs)
12. Documentation and release

=== PROMPT ===
Implement a line-by-line tokenizer for sws.

The tokenizer splits input lines into an array of token strings. It must
handle the following:

1. Bare words — split on whitespace (space, tab)
2. Quoted strings — "..." preserves whitespace inside, strips outer quotes
3. Brace blocks — { ... } with nested brace counting. The entire block
   including outer braces becomes one token. Track brace depth to handle:
   - { simple block }
   - { nested { inner } block }
   - Multi-line blocks (continue reading lines until braces balance)
4. Comments — # to end of line, stripped during tokenization
5. Empty lines — skip silently
6. Newlines — each line is one command (unless braces span lines)

Data structures (parallel arrays, no structs):
- token_buf[MAX_TOKENS][MAX_TOKEN_LEN] — token string storage
- token_count — number of tokens in current line
- token_type[MAX_TOKENS] — token type (WORD, QUOTED, BLOCK, etc.)

Implement:
- tokenize_line(line) → fills token arrays, returns token count
- Handle unterminated quotes/braces as syntax errors
- Read from input buffer or UART line-by-line

Test with:
- "echo hello world" → 3 tokens: echo, hello, world
- 'set x "hello world"' → 3 tokens: set, x, hello world
- "if {eq $x 0} { echo yes }" → tokens for command
- "echo test # comment" → 2 tokens: echo, test
- Multi-line brace block handling

Reference: docs/research.txt (tokenizer design section)

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test.sh -- Build and verify sws prints its banner
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

BUILD_DIR="build"
ASM="$BUILD_DIR/sws.s"
COR24_RUN="cor24-run"

# Build first
./scripts/build.sh build

echo "=== Testing sws banner ==="

OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$ASM" 2>&1)

if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "sws 0.1"; then
    echo "PASS: banner found"
    echo "  Output: $OUTPUT"
else
    echo "FAIL: expected 'sws 0.1' in output"
    echo "  Got: $OUTPUT"
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# build.sh -- Build sws interpreter for COR24
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/build.sh              Build only (compile to assembly)
#   ./scripts/build.sh run          Build and run on emulator
#   ./scripts/build.sh clean        Remove build artifacts
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

BUILD_DIR="build"
SRC="src/sws.c"
ASM="$BUILD_DIR/sws.s"

TC24R="tc24r"
TC24R_INCLUDE="/Users/mike/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-x-tinyc/include"
COR24_RUN="cor24-run"

build() {
    mkdir -p "$BUILD_DIR"
    echo "=== Compiling sws ==="
    "$TC24R" "$SRC" -o "$ASM" -I "$TC24R_INCLUDE"
    echo "  $SRC -> $ASM"
    echo ""
}

run() {
    build
    echo "=== Running on COR24 emulator ==="
    echo ""
    "$COR24_RUN" --run "$ASM" "$@"
}

clean() {
    rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR"
    echo "Cleaned build artifacts."
}

CMD="${1:-build}"

case "$CMD" in
    build)
        build
        echo "Build OK."
        ;;
    run)
        shift
        run "$@"
        ;;
    clean)
        clean
        ;;
    *)
        echo "Usage: $0 {build|run|clean}"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

cor24-run: COR24 assembler and emulator

Usage:
  cor24-run --demo [options]        Run built-in LED demo
  cor24-run --run <file.s> [opts]   Assemble and run
  cor24-run --load-binary <f>@<a> --entry <a>  Run pre-assembled binaries
  cor24-run --assemble <in.s> <out.bin> <out.lst>

Options:
  -h                     Short help (this message)
  --help                 Extended help with AI agent guidance
  -V, --version          Version, copyright, license, build info
  --speed, -s <ips>      Instructions per second (default: 100000)
  --time, -t <secs>      Time limit in seconds (default: 10)
  --max-instructions, -n <count>  Stop after N instructions (-1 = no limit)
  --uart-input, -u <str> Send characters to UART RX (supports \n, \x21)
  --entry, -e <label|addr> Set entry point (label name or numeric address)
  --dump                 Dump CPU state, I/O, and non-zero memory after halt
  --trace <N>            Dump last N instructions on halt/timeout (default: 50)
  --step                 Print each instruction as it executes
  --terminal             Bridge stdin/stdout to UART (interactive mode)
  --echo                 Local echo in terminal mode (for programs that don't echo)
  --load-binary <file>@<addr>  Load raw bytes into memory at address
  --patch <addr>=<value> Write 24-bit value to memory (repeatable)
  --base-addr <addr>     Base address for assembly (default: 0)
  --stack-kilobytes <3|8>  EBR stack size (default: 3, max: 8)
  --switch <on|off>      Set button S2 state (default: off/released)
  --uart-never-ready     UART TX stays busy forever (test polling)

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test.sh -- Build and verify sws tokenizer
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

BUILD_DIR="build"
ASM="$BUILD_DIR/sws.s"
COR24_RUN="cor24-run"

# Build first
./scripts/build.sh build

PASS=0
FAIL=0

run_test() {
    local desc="$1"
    local input="$2"
    local expected="$3"

    OUTPUT=$("$COR24_RUN" --run "$ASM" -u "$input" -t 5 2>&1)

    if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qF "$expected"; then
        echo "PASS: $desc"
        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
    else
        echo "FAIL: $desc"
        echo "  Expected: $expected"
        echo "  Got: $OUTPUT"
        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
    fi
}

echo "=== Testing sws tokenizer ==="

# Banner test
run_test "banner" "\x04" "sws 0.1"

# Bare words
run_test "bare words" "echo hello world\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|echo] [WORD|hello] [WORD|world]"

# Quoted string
run_test "quoted string" 'set x "hello world"\n\x04' \
    "[WORD|set] [WORD|x] [QUOTED|hello world]"

# Brace block
run_test "brace block" "if cond { echo yes }\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|if] [WORD|cond] [BLOCK|{ echo yes }]"

# Comment stripping
run_test "comment stripping" "echo test # comment\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|echo] [WORD|test]"

# Comment-only line (no token output, just prompt)
run_test "comment-only line" "# just a comment\necho ok\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|echo] [WORD|ok]"

# Multiple spaces
run_test "multiple spaces" "echo    hello\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|echo] [WORD|hello]"

# Nested braces
run_test "nested braces" "if cond { if inner { echo yes } }\n\x04" \
    "[WORD|if] [WORD|cond] [BLOCK|{ if inner { echo yes } }]"

echo ""
echo "=== Results: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
    exit 1
fi

# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.

feat(tokenizer): add line tokenizer with bare words, quotes, braces, and comments

Implement tokenize_line() that splits input into typed tokens (WORD, QUOTED,
BLOCK). Uses a flat token_pool with index array instead of 2D arrays, which
tc24r/as24 cannot handle (immediate 256 exceeds lc range). Supports:
- Bare words split on whitespace
- "quoted strings" with backslash escapes (\n, \t, \\)
- { brace blocks } with nested brace counting
- # comment stripping
- Syntax error reporting for unterminated quotes/braces

Main loop now reads lines, tokenizes, and echoes [TYPE|value] debug output.
Static limits reduced to fit COR24 assembler constraints (MAX_TOKENS=32,
MAX_TOKEN_LEN=128). Test suite expanded from 1 banner test to 8 cases
covering all token types.
